topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of stale coffee and old paper usually brought a sense of comfortable familiarity to my room, but today, it was suffocating. I walked in to find the light from the desk lamp casting an unnatural glow over my open journal. My breath hitched, lodging somewhere tight in my chest. My eyes darted from the ink on the page—my deepest, most private thoughts laid bare—to the corner of the room where Liam was sitting, ostensibly studying, but his gaze was fixed, intensely focused, on the open book. When he finally looked up, a flicker of something unreadable crossed his face, and I felt a cold wave wash over me, making my hands shake uncontrollably as I tried to snatch the book away.

[story 2]

It started subtly. A misplaced bookmark, a slightly different arrangement of my scattered notes. Then, one evening, I found my diary open to a page describing a dream I’d only shared with myself. My heart began to pound against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat against my sternum. I crept to the door, my movements slow and deliberate, like something fragile about to shatter. When I peered around the frame, Liam was there, leaning over my desk, his expression completely blank, as if he were merely observing an interesting artifact. The air in the room seemed to thicken, pressing down on my lungs, leaving me unable to draw a full breath.

[story 3]

I found the evidence tucked beneath my pillow: my journal, its pages slightly dog-eared, a passage I’d written weeks ago highlighted with a faint, almost invisible smudge of something dark. A sudden, sharp intake of air stole the sound from my throat. I stood frozen by the door, my entire body rigid, every muscle coiled tight. I could hear the faint rustle of paper, the soft turning of a page, coming from the direction of Liam’s side of the room. My vision narrowed, focusing solely on the gap between the door and him, a vast, impossible distance separating my safety from whatever silent scrutiny was taking place. I didn't move; I couldn't seem to force my feet to shift.
